title: On Heaven.
content:
Permit mine eyes to see
    Part, or the whole of Thee,
    O happy place!
    Where all have grace,
    And garlands shar'd,
    For their reward;
    Where each chaste soul
    In long white stole,
    And palms in hand,
    Do ravish'd stand;
    So in a ring,
    The praises sing
    Of Three in One
    That fill the Throne;
    While harps and viols then
    To voices say, Amen.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "On Heaven."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
